pages.title: Project Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to the Create_penntree_feature_for_TF project: its goals, the datasets it bridges, and how the pieces fit together. Links to child pages for setup and background context.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for setting up the environment, installing dependencies (Text-Fabric, NLTK, MACULA XML source), loading the N1904 corpus, and running the notebooks for the first time.

pages.title: Background: Penn Treebank and Text-Fabric
pages.purpose: Explains the two foundational frameworks the project depends on — the Penn Treebank (PTB) labeled-bracketing convention and the Text-Fabric (TF) graph-based corpus model — and why they are combined here.

pages.title: Core Architecture: The Feature Generation Pipeline
pages.purpose: Overview of the end-to-end data pipeline that transforms MACULA XML source data into the penntree TF feature. Introduces the three stages (extraction, tree construction, serialization) and links to child pages for each.

pages.title: XML Parsing and the TagTracker State Machine
pages.purpose: Deep dive into how the MACULA Greek New Testament XML is parsed. Covers the TagTracker class, its stack-based depth-first traversal, the start_element and end_element callbacks, and how sentence, wg, and w tags are handled.
pages.parent: Core Architecture: The Feature Generation Pipeline

pages.title: MACULA-to-PTB Schema Mapping
pages.purpose: Documents the translation layer between MACULA Greek syntactic roles/classes and standard Penn Treebank phrase labels and function tags. Covers phraseRole(), class-to-label mapping tables, POS uppercasing, and known edge cases such as post-positive conjunctions.
pages.parent: Core Architecture: The Feature Generation Pipeline

pages.title: Tree Serialization and Punctuation Injection
pages.purpose: Explains how the nested parenthesis string is assembled during traversal, how punctuation embedded in the 'after' XML attribute is extracted and injected as explicit PUNCT nodes, and how unicode normalization (NFC) is applied to Greek text.
pages.parent: Core Architecture: The Feature Generation Pipeline

pages.title: Consistency Checking and Validation
pages.purpose: Describes the two-layer validation system: the custom annotate_parentheses / check_consistency parenthesis-balance checker, and the final NLTK Tree.fromstring() round-trip validation run against all 8,011 generated sentence trees.
pages.parent: Core Architecture: The Feature Generation Pipeline

pages.title: Output: The penntree Text-Fabric Feature
pages.purpose: Overview of the penntree feature as a TF artifact — its file format, metadata header, node-ID alignment, and how it integrates with the N1904addons distribution. Links to child pages covering the file format and the examples notebook.

pages.title: penntree.tf File Format and Metadata
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for the tf/1.0.0/penntree.tf file: the TF binary header fields (valueType, version, writtenBy, description, license), the node-ID-to-string data layout, the sentence node offset (258525), and how the file is written via TF.save().

pages.title: Using the penntree Feature: Examples Notebook
pages.purpose: Walkthrough of examples.ipynb — how to load N1904-TF together with the N1904addons dataset, retrieve a penntree string with F.penntree.v(sentenceNode), and visualize the result with nltk.Tree.fromstring() and tree.pretty_print().

pages.title: Repository Assets and Licensing
pages.purpose: Covers the non-code assets in the repository: the CC BY 4.0 license, the images directory (license badge), and the .gitignore configuration. Links to child pages for license details and asset management.

pages.title: License and Attribution
pages.purpose: Full details of the CC BY 4.0 license governing the notebook and output feature files, required attribution text and URL (https://github.com/tonyjurg/N1904addons), upstream dataset licenses (N1904-TF MIT, Nestle 1904 public domain), and citation guidance.

pages.title: Repository Structure and Development Environment
pages.purpose: Describes the repository layout (notebooks, tf/ output directory, images/ assets), the .gitignore rules that exclude Jupyter checkpoint directories, and guidance for contributors on keeping the repo clean.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, abbreviations, and domain concepts that an onboarding engineer needs to understand. Each entry includes code pointers to the relevant file or function.
